title: Provisional Eva Unit 05
wikitext:
[]: Provisional Eva Unit 05 (spoilers) Post by FLCL2 » Tue Jul 28, 2009 12:59 pm I was doing some thinking about the intricate piloting setup that Mari has to use to pilot Eva 05, and after doing some thinking, I think I've kind of figured out how it works. Eva 05 is a mix of organic and cybernetic components. The main cybernetic components being the arms and legs. To me, these seem to be connected to the green tubes that go into the arms on her plugsuit. Two cybernetic arms and four cybernetic legs = 6 cybernetic components. There are also 6 tubes that are hooked up to Mari. I think that she controls the arms and legs somehow via these tubes, most likely they are interfaced directly into her neural patterns. Also, when Tunniel blasts her legs, you can see her right arm burning, and when her legs are blasted off, you can see that two of the tubes on her right are gone, which to me indicates that when the connections to the cybernetic legs were severed, the tubes just popped off. The tubes seem to be filled with LCL, since when the last tube on her right arm pops off, you can see what looks like LCL that hasn't been ionized yet coming out of it. As to why the final tubes popped off, I assume that Mari was letting her Eva enter sort of a controlled berserk state, letting the Eva itself take control of its own cybernetic components, and her pushing forward on the left control handle was her way of willing the Eva to do what it had to do. (I could possibly word that better.) As for the control handles, these have numerous buttons on it, and I think these buttons are for more complex actions, like switching from external to battery power, or for activating the Eva's jet boosters. They may also interface with the helmet that Mari wears. Which brings me to the helmet. This seems to be for nothing more than monitoring the Eva's status, functions and systems, as well as providing a detailed view of her surroundings. I could be completely wrong about this, but this is my theory. Comments and criticisms, and perhaps revisions/corrections are welcome.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jul 28, 2009 1:48 pm Seems like a very logical theory, very nice. I believe some other people have said something like this but good job none the less. I still wish to know whos soul is inside Unit 05 and how it goes into a berserker state for a sec.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jul 28, 2009 2:27 pm Probably Mari's mother? Dur?

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:40 am AuraTwilight wrote: Probably Mari's mother? Dur?

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 29, 2009 5:35 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: I still wish to know whos soul is inside Unit 05 and how it goes into a berserker state for a sec.

[]: Post by LiLi » Wed Jul 29, 2009 6:13 am Reichu wrote: Some people (including me) think she's giving control of the right arm to the Eva (hence detaching the only remaining tube on the right, followed shortly by the Eva's jaws opening), others are pretty stuck to the notion that she's just talking about how she leaves the lance part of the arm in the Angel. No contest here for me.

[]: Post by master_lloyd » Wed Jul 29, 2009 6:33 am LiLi wrote: :EDIT: So, what do the folks here think that controller Mari pushes against with all her might does?

[]: Post by LiLi » Wed Jul 29, 2009 6:36 am master_lloyd wrote: Windscreen wiper.

[]: Post by master_lloyd » Wed Jul 29, 2009 6:49 am Excuse my flippancy. The controls in Evas are curious things, despite them being generally thought-controlled there are little switches and triggers for things like firing guns. I doubt there's a proper use for the thing she was pushing against, it's probably only there as an easy way for the animators to show she's really trying...

[]: Post by LiLi » Wed Jul 29, 2009 6:51 am master_lloyd wrote: Excuse my flippancy. The controls in Evas are curious things, despite them being generally thought-controlled there are little switches and trigger for things like firing guns. I doubt there's a proper use for the thing she was pushing against, it's probably only there as an easy way for the animators to show she's really trying...

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 29, 2009 7:00 am master_lloyd wrote: Also wasn't there a Unit-05 thread already going in the run-up to 2.0? What happened to that?

[]: Post by LiLi » Wed Jul 29, 2009 7:09 am On a side note, I don't know if this was mentioned already somewhere else, but from what Kaji said it seems Eva 05 was put together in a hurry. Mari refers to it as provisional (仮設) - but Kaji actually apologizes to Mari for sending her into battle on an "improvised" (急造品) unit (in my understanding).

[]: Post by master_lloyd » Wed Jul 29, 2009 7:12 am Reichu wrote: It was an awkward combo Eva-05/Eva-06 thread. http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6249

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 29, 2009 10:37 am AuraTwilight wrote: Probably Mari's mother? Dur?

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Jul 29, 2009 10:42 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: I still wish to know whos soul is inside Unit 05 and how it goes into a berserker state for a sec.

[]: Post by wonderluster » Wed Jul 29, 2009 11:30 am You know there is the fact that since Eva is a living being, and being proven that it has a brain, it may have a mind. Maybe not a soul but a slight conscious, thus it may have been partially aware of what's going on and I said something similar about Unit 02 in he beast mode thread but if the pilot is conscious while the berserk state is induced then maybe the "beast" can see what's going on but has no control over its body, a voodoo of sorts where the pilot is the puppet master. When Mari says " you can have this arm" I would defiantly suppose that is means that she knows that unit 05 is aware and thus is giving the arm to it. I also have rights to believe that Eva unit 05 didn't have a core either due to all these posts saying it didn't have a soul. However if it did have a core then maybe that would have been the soul of the Eva itself? I dunno, just throwin my thoughts out.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:24 pm Eva's do not come pre-packed with a soul. Maybe Rebuild is different that way. I believe they have a "will" but they are simply a vessel without a soul.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:39 pm Difficult to speculate about technical minutiae now, since we can't see most of the computer screens too well, and the background technobabble is essentially incoherent. Eva-05's "core unit" might be mentioned somewhere in there. Given current lack of information, the coreless/soulless speculation is probably unnecessary, but... whatever. wonderluster wrote: However if it did have a core then maybe that would have been the soul of the Eva itself?

[]: Post by wonderluster » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:40 pm Ok, Eva's are living beings and all living beings some from something right? I mean humans give birth to humans and how does that work in the soul system? Part of the mothers soul detaches and develops on its own? I think that if Evas are derived from angels and humans, then they must have souls, as do the Angels and the Humans.

[]: Post by LiLi » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:50 pm wonderluster wrote: I think that if Evas are derived from angels and humans, then they must have souls, as do the Angels and the Humans.
